title: Lean Out Of The Window
content:
Lean out of the window,
    Goldenhair,
    I hear you singing
    A merry air.

    My book was closed,
    I read no more,
    Watching the fire dance
    On the floor.

    I have left my book,
    I have left my room,
    For I heard you singing
    Through the gloom.

    Singing and singing
    A merry air,
    Lean out of the window,
    Goldenhair.
meaning:
This evocative piece by James Joyce, titled "Lean Out Of The Window", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
